rows.title: Full 3D Global FEA Core

linear_solver_refinement.claim_boundary: Refines the regularized free-DOF linear subproblem solve and keeps the lowest residual iterate. It is not a substitute for a consistent nonlinear Newton/Jacobian.

full_frame_6dof_deformed_state_pdelta_pa.claim_boundary: Deformed-coordinate 6-DOF frame P-Delta fixed-point path under scaled gravity. It is geometric nonlinear frame evidence, not full material nonlinear or shell/plate closure.

full_frame_6dof_linear_solver_refinement.claim_boundary: Refines the regularized free-DOF linear subproblem solve and keeps the lowest residual iterate. It is not a substitute for a consistent nonlinear Newton/Jacobian.

pdelta_adaptive_micro_continuation_probe.claim_boundary: Bounded adaptive micro-step continuation above the last direct converged load. It can promote accepted micro-steps into the continuation path, but it is not full-load closure unless the target load is reached.

pdelta_post_failed_relaxation_sensitivit.claim_boundary: Relaxation sensitivity at the first failed post-accepted micro-step. This tests whether a more conservative fixed-point update can recover the path, but it is not closure unless both the residual and unrelaxed fixed-point increment meet tolerance.

pdelta_secant_predictor_probe.claim_boundary: Secant displacement predictor from two accepted P-Delta states. It tests whether a path predictor can improve the failed next micro-step. It is not closure unless one row passes both residual and unrelaxed fixed-point increment gates.

predictor.claim_boundary: Secant displacement predictor from two accepted P-Delta states. It tests whether a path predictor can improve the failed next micro-step. It is not closure unless one row passes both residual and unrelaxed fixed-point increment gates.

Secant displacement predictor from two accepted P-Delta states. It tests whether a path predictor can improve the failed next micro-step. It is not closure unless one row passes both residual and unrelaxed fixed-point increment gates.

pdelta_secant_micro_continuation_probe.claim_boundary: Bounded secant-predictor micro-continuation after the first accepted secant step. It can advance the accepted P-Delta frontier, but it is not full-load closure unless the target load is reached and every accepted row passes both residual and unrelaxed fixed-point gates.

predictor.claim_boundary: Secant displacement predictor from two accepted P-Delta states. It tests whether a path predictor can improve the failed next micro-step. It is not closure unless one row passes both residual and unrelaxed fixed-point increment gates.

pdelta_fine_secant_micro_continuation_pr.claim_boundary: Bounded secant-predictor micro-continuation after the first accepted secant step. It can advance the accepted P-Delta frontier, but it is not full-load closure unless the target load is reached and every accepted row passes both residual and unrelaxed fixed-point gates.

pdelta_frontier_diagnostic.claim_boundary: Derived from existing P-Delta continuation rows. This clarifies the accepted frontier and the next failed gate, but it is not new nonlinear equilibrium closure evidence.

pdelta_frontier_residual_jacobian_summar.claim_boundary: Summarizes the existing frontier residual-Jacobian receipt. It is a compact gate-distance view only; it does not add new nonlinear equilibrium closure evidence.

pdelta_frontier_residual_jacobian_probe.claim_boundary: Finite-difference residual-Jacobian probe at the nearest failed P-Delta frontier bracket. It builds a small Newton-Krylov correction from tangent, fixed-point, and load-path directions and accepts only after both direct residual and remapped fixed-point increment gates pass. It is a local frontier closure only, not full-load material/shell Newton closure.

pdelta_first_failed_one_step_line_search.claim_boundary: One-step approximate Newton correction with residual-based alpha scan at the first failed continuation load. This is diagnostic evidence for the consistent Newton/Jacobian gap, not a full nonlinear path closure.

pdelta_first_failed_anderson_acceleratio.label: constrained_anderson
pdelta_first_failed_anderson_acceleratio.claim_boundary: Constrained Anderson acceleration on the existing deformed-state P-Delta fixed-point map. Optional coefficient L1 limiting blends unstable extrapolation back toward the latest mapped state. This is a path-following diagnostic only; it does not provide a consistent Newton/Jacobian or material Newton closure.

pdelta_first_failed_coefficient_bounded_.label: coefficient_bounded_anderson_l1_8
pdelta_first_failed_coefficient_bounded_.claim_boundary: Constrained Anderson acceleration on the existing deformed-state P-Delta fixed-point map. Optional coefficient L1 limiting blends unstable extrapolation back toward the latest mapped state. This is a path-following diagnostic only; it does not provide a consistent Newton/Jacobian or material Newton closure.

pdelta_first_failed_residual_trust_regio.label: residual_trust_region_anderson
pdelta_first_failed_residual_trust_regio.claim_boundary: Residual-trust-region Anderson probe. Unlike damped update diagnostics, every candidate is remapped through the P-Delta fixed-point operator and closure requires the remapped true fixed-point relative increment, residual, and displacement gates to pass.

direct_residual_newton_direction.linearized_tangent: current service-material frame tangent plus frame geometric delta plus shell tangent plus finite springs

linear_solver_refinement.claim_boundary: Refines the regularized free-DOF linear subproblem solve and keeps the lowest residual iterate. It is not a substitute for a consistent nonlinear Newton/Jacobian.

uncoarsened_boundary_pdelta_checkpoint_r.claim_boundary: Accepted displacement states can be written and replayed as exact seeds for later frontier probes. This reduces rerun cost for bracket search, but it does not change the nonlinear convergence gate or promote a failed residual/increment state.

checkpoint_resume.claim_boundary: Accepted displacement states can be written and replayed as exact seeds for later frontier probes. This reduces rerun cost for bracket search, but it does not change the nonlinear convergence gate or promote a failed residual/increment state.

uncoarsened_boundary_pdelta_checkpoint_r.claim_boundary: This probe wires the uncoarsened authored support masks and finite elastic-link spring tangent into a deformed-coordinate frame-shell P-Delta fixed-point loop. The frame tangent uses geometric stiffness from frame gravity axial forces and the shell tangent is rebuilt on the deformed nodal coordinates. It is a nonlinear path diagnostic only; it is not a consistent Newton/Jacobian, material nonlinear, or full-load closure.

secant_seed.claim_boundary: Secant prediction only changes the initial displacement seed for the next load step. The step is promoted only if the existing residual and unrelaxed fixed-point increment gates pass after remapping through the P-Delta operator.

seed_alpha_scan.claim_boundary: Seed alpha scan is a one-step globalization diagnostic. It builds candidates along the first fixed-point correction direction and remaps each candidate through the same P-Delta operator. It is closure only if the remapped residual and unrelaxed fixed-point gates pass.

pdelta_post_failed_relaxation_sensitivit.claim_boundary: Relaxation sensitivity at the first failed post-accepted micro-step. This tests whether a more conservative fixed-point update can recover the path, but it is not closure unless both the residual and unrelaxed fixed-point increment meet tolerance.

pdelta_secant_predictor_probe.claim_boundary: Secant displacement predictor from two accepted P-Delta states. It tests whether a path predictor can improve the failed next micro-step. It is not closure unless one row passes both residual and unrelaxed fixed-point increment gates.

pdelta_frontier_residual_jacobian_summar.claim_boundary: Summarizes the existing frontier residual-Jacobian receipt. It is a compact gate-distance view only; it does not add new nonlinear equilibrium closure evidence.

pdelta_frontier_residual_jacobian_probe.claim_boundary: Finite-difference residual-Jacobian probe at the nearest failed P-Delta frontier bracket. It builds a small Newton-Krylov correction from tangent, fixed-point, and load-path directions and accepts only after both direct residual and remapped fixed-point increment gates pass. It is a local frontier closure only, not full-load material/shell Newton closure.

pdelta_first_failed_one_step_line_search.claim_boundary: One-step approximate Newton correction with residual-based alpha scan at the first failed continuation load. This is diagnostic evidence for the consistent Newton/Jacobian gap, not a full nonlinear path closure.

This is a path-following diagnostic only; it does not provide a consistent Newton/Jacobian or material Newton closure.
